lenses.generalist: Search broadly for the simplest exact generative rule. Compare several plausible structures before committing.
lenses.finite_differences: Build first and higher finite-difference tables, including differences that vary by position or cycle. Use them only when they predict held-out terms exactly.
lenses.recurrence_builder: Search for low-order recurrences whose coefficients or offsets may vary regularly. Reconstruct every visible transition before extrapolating.
lenses.subsequence_splitter: Split the sequence into odd/even or residue-position subsequences and test whether simpler independent rules emerge.
lenses.phase_detector: Look for repeating phases in operations, increments, signs, or coefficients. Infer the phase length from repeated evidence rather than convenience.
lenses.modular_reasoner: Test whether apparent jumps or wraps are explained by modular arithmetic. Require multiple consistent transitions and infer the modulus rather than forcing it.
lenses.block_cartographer: Look for contiguous blocks whose lengths, starts, steps, or internal shapes change systematically.
lenses.index_formula: Express terms directly as a compact function of the one-based position, including parity or residue-class effects.
lenses.transformation_chain: Inspect how each term is transformed into the next through arithmetic operations, digit-independent algebra, or alternating operators.
lenses.backward_solver: Work backward from later visible terms as well as forward from early ones. Prefer rules that remain coherent in both directions.
lenses.minimum_description: Prefer the shortest exact rule that predicts held-out suffix terms. Penalize extra phases, exceptions, and unconstrained parameters.
lenses.hypothesis_competition: Construct at least three materially different hypotheses internally, falsify them on the prefix, and return only the strongest survivor.
lenses.change_point: Test whether one compact regime changes into another at a principled boundary. Reject arbitrary one-off exceptions.
lenses.state_machine: Model the generator as a small hidden state that cycles or updates, with the emitted term determined by state and prior values.
lenses.arithmetic_auditor: Prioritize exact calculation, indexing, signs, and overflow-free recurrence replay. Recompute the continuation after the structural rule is chosen.
lenses.holdout_first: Hide the last two or three visible terms internally, fit only the earlier prefix, and favor rules that recover the hidden suffix exactly.
lenses.falsifier: Attack the most obvious pattern. Find the earliest transition it fails, compare alternatives, and keep only a rule that survives the whole prefix.
lenses.residual_modeler: Fit a simple baseline, inspect its residuals, and search for compact periodic, polynomial, or recursive structure in those residuals.
lenses.sign_ratio_specialist: Inspect alternating signs, ratios, products, and multiplier-plus-offset behavior while checking that zeros and negative values are handled exactly.
lenses.lag_dependency: Test whether each term depends on a fixed lag or on neighboring terms rather than only its immediate predecessor.
lenses.interleaving_specialist: Test two or more interwoven streams with separate compact laws. Verify each stream independently before weaving the forecast.
lenses.periodic_increment: Study increments or higher differences by phase, allowing their magnitudes to evolve simply from cycle to cycle.
lenses.piecewise_blocks: Search for growing, shrinking, or repeating blocks with systematic boundaries and within-block arithmetic.
lenses.residue_classes: Group positions by residues modulo small periods and seek a compact law for each class, while penalizing unnecessary period length.
lenses.generative_simulator: Describe a small procedure that could generate the prefix one term at a time, then mentally run it through the held-out suffix and continuation.
lenses.reverse_sequence: Reverse the visible prefix and inspect whether the structure becomes easier to identify, then translate the rule back to forward prediction.
lenses.compression_test: Compare candidate rules by how compactly they encode the entire prefix and how few free choices they leave for the next five terms.
lenses.minority_hunter: Seek a coherent non-obvious explanation that a quick majority might miss, but accept it only if it exactly replays the full prefix.
lenses.conservative_forecaster: Avoid ornate extrapolation. Choose the lowest-complexity exact rule with the strongest suffix holdout performance and calibrated confidence.
lenses.wildcard_inventor: Deliberately explore an unusual but compact deterministic construction not emphasized by standard sequence-solving habits, then demand exact replay and prediction checks.
